purpose.summary: The COPAUS1C program is a CICS transaction that displays authorization details and allows users to mark authorizations as fraudulent. It receives input from a calling program via the COMMAREA, displays an authorization view screen (COPAU1A), and allows the user to navigate through authorizations and mark them as fraudulent, linking to another program (WS-PGM-AUTH-FRAUD) to update the fraud details.
purpose.business_context: This program is used to view and manage authorization details, specifically to identify and report fraudulent authorizations.

inputs.name: DFHCOMMAREA
inputs.description: Communication area passed from the calling program, containing account ID, authorization key, and other context information.

inputs.name: COPAU1AI
inputs.description: Input map structure received from the CICS terminal, containing user input from the COPAU1A screen.

outputs.name: COPAU1AO
outputs.description: Output map structure sent to the CICS terminal, containing authorization details and header information for the COPAU1A screen.

outputs.name: WS-FRAUD-DATA
outputs.description: Communication area passed to the WS-PGM-AUTH-FRAUD program, containing fraud details.

called_programs.purpose: Transfers control back to the calling program (CDEMO) after processing or when the user presses PF3.
Links to a program that handles fraud reporting and updates.

business_rules.description: If the account ID is numeric and an authorization key is selected, read the authorization record.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks if CDEMO-ACCT-ID is numeric and CDEMO-CPVD-PAU-SELECTED is not spaces or low-values. If both conditions are true, READ-AUTH-RECORD is performed.

business_rules.description: If the authorization is already marked as fraudulent, remove the fraud flag; otherwise, set the fraud flag.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks if PA-FRAUD-CONFIRMED is set. If true, sets PA-FRAUD-REMOVED to true and WS-REMOVE-FRAUD to true. Otherwise, sets PA-FRAUD-CONFIRMED to true and WS-REPORT-FRAUD to true.

business_rules.description: Determine authorization response status based on PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE.
business_rules.logic_summary: If PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE is '00', the authorization is approved, and AUTHRSPO is set to 'A' with a green color. Otherwise, the authorization is declined, and AUTHRSPO is set to 'D' with a red color.

transforms.transformation_description: Formats the authorization date from YYMMDD to MM/DD/YY.
Formats the authorization time from HHMMSS to HH:MM:SS.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the communication area passed between CICS programs in the CARDDEMO application.
Defines the map structure for the COPAU1A screen.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the program ID. It does not contain any executable code.

This paragraph serves as the main control flow for the COPAUS1C program. It first initializes the error flag and message. It checks if the COMMAREA is empty (EIBCALEN = 0), indicating a first-time call, and initializes the CARDDEMO-COMMAREA, moves the program ID to CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM, and performs RETURN-TO-PREV-SCREEN to return to the calling program. Otherwise, it moves the DFHCOMMAREA to CARDDEMO-COMMAREA and checks if the program is re-entered. If not, it sets the re-enter flag and performs PROCESS-ENTER-KEY and SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. If it is a re-entry, it receives the screen input, evaluates the EIBAID (AID key pressed), and performs different actions based on the key pressed, such as processing the ENTER key, PF3 (returning to the previous screen), PF5 (marking authorization as fraud), or PF8 (processing the next authorization). Finally, it returns control to CICS with the updated COMMAREA.

This paragraph processes the ENTER key press. It initializes the COPAU1AO map. If the account ID (CDEMO-ACCT-ID) is numeric and an authorization key (CDEMO-CPVD-PAU-SELECTED) is selected, it moves the account ID and authorization key to working storage variables (WS-ACCT-ID, WS-AUTH-KEY) and performs READ-AUTH-RECORD to retrieve the authorization details. If IMS-PSB-SCHD is set, it performs TAKE-SYNCPOINT. If the account ID is not numeric or no authorization key is selected, it sets the error flag. Finally, it performs POPULATE-AUTH-DETAILS to populate the screen fields with the authorization details.

This paragraph handles the process of marking an authorization as fraudulent. It moves the account ID and authorization key to working storage. It then performs READ-AUTH-RECORD to retrieve the authorization details. If the authorization is already marked as fraud confirmed, it removes the fraud flag; otherwise, it sets the fraud flag. It moves the pending authorization details and account/customer IDs to the WS-FRAUD-DATA area. It then links to the WS-PGM-AUTH-FRAUD program to update the fraud details. If the link is successful and the fraud update is successful, it performs UPDATE-AUTH-DETAILS. Otherwise, it moves an error message and performs ROLL-BACK. If the link fails, it performs ROLL-BACK. Finally, it moves the authorization key to CDEMO-CPVD-PAU-SELECTED and performs POPULATE-AUTH-DETAILS to refresh the screen.

This paragraph handles the processing when the PF8 key is pressed, which is intended to display the next authorization record. It moves the account ID and authorization key from the COMMAREA to working storage. It then performs READ-AUTH-RECORD to read the current authorization record and READ-NEXT-AUTH-RECORD to read the next authorization record. If IMS-PSB-SCHD is set, it performs TAKE-SYNCPOINT. If the end of file (AUTHS-EOF) is reached, it sets SEND-ERASE-NO to TRUE and moves a message indicating that the last authorization has been reached to WS-MESSAGE. Otherwise, it moves the authorization key to CDEMO-CPVD-PAU-SELECTED and performs POPULATE-AUTH-DETAILS to display the next authorization details.

This paragraph populates the authorization details on the screen (COPAU1AO) using data from the PA- (Pending Authorization) fields. It moves the card number, authorization date (formatted), authorization time (formatted), and approved amount to the corresponding output fields. It determines the authorization response status ('A' for Approved, 'D' for Declined) based on the PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE and sets the corresponding color. It searches the WS-DECLINE-REASON-TAB to find the description for the decline reason code. It moves other authorization details like processing code, POS entry mode, message source, merchant category code, card expiry date, authorization type, transaction ID, match status, fraud information, and merchant information to the output map. This paragraph is responsible for formatting and displaying the authorization information on the screen.

This paragraph prepares to return to the calling program (CDEMO). It moves the current transaction ID (WS-CICS-TRANID) and program ID (WS-PGM-AUTH-DTL) to the CARDDEMO-COMMAREA. It sets the program context to ZEROS and sets the CDEMO-PGM-ENTER flag to TRUE. It then performs a CICS XCTL to transfer control to the program specified in CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM, passing the CARDDEMO-COMMAREA.

This paragraph sends the COPAU1A screen to the terminal. It first performs POPULATE-HEADER-INFO to populate the header information on the screen. It moves the message in WS-MESSAGE to the error message field (ERRMSGO) on the screen. It moves -1 to CARDNUML to set the cursor position. If SEND-ERASE-YES is set, it sends the map with the ERASE option, clearing the screen before sending the map. Otherwise, it sends the map without the ERASE option, overlaying the new data on the existing screen.

This paragraph receives data from the COPAU1A screen. It executes a CICS RECEIVE command to receive the data from the map into the COPAU1AI structure. NOHANDLE is specified to suppress exception handling.

This paragraph populates the header information on the COPAU1A screen. It moves the current date to WS-CURDATE-DATA using the FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE. It moves the title fields (CCDA-TITLE01, CCDA-TITLE02), transaction name (WS-CICS-TRANID), and program name (WS-PGM-AUTH-DTL) to the corresponding output fields in the COPAU1AO map. It formats the current date and time into the required output formats.

error_handling.condition: EIBRESP NOT = DFHRESP(NORMAL) during LINK to WS-PGM-AUTH-FRAUD
WS-FRD-UPDT-SUCCESS is false after LINK to WS-PGM-AUTH-FRAUD
error_handling.action: MOVE WS-FRD-ACT-MSG to WS-MESSAGE and ROLL-BACK is performed.

cics_operations.purpose: Transfers control to another CICS program.
Sends the COPAU1A map to the terminal.
Receives data from the COPAU1A map.
Links to the fraud processing program.
